name: Arrábida Park and Sesimbra Loop
name_original: Percurso circular 1 de Sesimbra — Parque Natural da Serra da Arrábida
description: This difficult 33.3-mile touring cycling route in Arrábida Natural Park offers stunning coastal views and significant climbs.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Santiago offers diverse landscapes, situated between the Andes Mountains and the Chilean Coastal Range. The region features varied terrain, including challenging mountain ascents, serene vineyard routes, and scenic paths along rivers and through canyons. Cyclists can explore high-altitude vistas, rural landscapes, and unique rock formations. This geographical position provides access to snow-capped peaks, Mediterranean forests, and coastal areas. Best touring cycling routes around Santiago The most popular touring cycling route is Arrábida Park and Sesimbra Loop, a 33.3 miles (53.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through a natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ouro Beach – Hermitage of Memory loop from Sesimbra, a difficult 18.8 miles (30.3 km) path. This route offers coastal views and climbs through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Nice gravel route loop from Castelo, a 22.5 miles (36.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Touring cycling around Santiago is defined by mountain ascents, vineyard paths, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Santiago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santiago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Santiago?
A: Santiago offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes. You can find challenging mountain ascents in the Andes, serene vineyard routes in the Maipo Valley, and scenic paths along rivers and through canyons. The region caters to various skill levels, from moderate rides to difficult routes with significant elevation gain.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Santiago area?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Santiago, with options ranging from easy to difficult. Specifically, you'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks I can expect to see while cycling around Santiago?
A: Cycling around Santiago offers stunning natural beauty. You can expect to see the majestic Andes Mountains, the picturesque Maipo Valley with its vineyards, and the dramatic Cajón del Maipo canyon, featuring rock formations, rivers like the Colorado, and the turquoise waters of the El Yeso Reservoir. Within the city, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4240053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Hill&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of Santiago and the Andes.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for challenging mountain cycling near Santiago?
A: Yes, the Andes Mountains provide significant challenges for avid cyclists. Destinations like Farellones and Valle Nevado offer routes with thousands of meters of ascent, providing impressive mountain vistas and opportunities to spot condors. The Cajón del Maipo also features mountain biking opportunities on hard-packed and gravel roads.

Q: Where can I find more relaxed touring cycling routes, perhaps with a scenic backdrop?
A: For more relaxed touring, the Maipo Valley, south of Santiago, is ideal. Here, you can cycle through vineyards, ancient tree-lined paths, and along the Maipo River, often with the Andes as a majestic backdrop. These routes offer a picturesque rural landscape perfect for a leisurely ride combined with wine tasting.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santiago?
A: The touring cycling routes in Santiago are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 32 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any urban cycling options or bike paths within Santiago itself?
A: Santiago has a growing network of urban bike paths and parks. Routes connect districts like Las Condes, Vitacura, and Providencia. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4240053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Hill&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Metropolitan Park, offers several routes to its summit with varying difficulty. The city also implements 'Ciclorecreovía' on Sundays, closing streets for cyclists and pedestrians.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santiago?
A: While the guide doesn't specify the absolute best time, Santiago's climate generally offers good conditions for cycling for much of the year. The Andes provide a stunning backdrop, and the Maipo Valley is pleasant. Consider the summer months for higher mountain routes, but be mindful of heat in lower elevations. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Santiago?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Santiago are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, routes exploring the Maipo Valley or ascending &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4240053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Hill&lt;/a&gt; often feature circular paths, providing varied scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: Can I find routes suitable for families or beginners in Santiago?
A: Yes, while Santiago is known for its challenging mountain routes, there are also options suitable for families and beginners. The region offers 1 easy route and 9 moderate routes. The urban bike paths and parks, as well as some flatter sections in the Maipo Valley, can be good starting points for less experienced cyclists or families.

Q: Are there any dedicated bike parks for mountain biking enthusiasts near Santiago?
A: Yes, for mountain biking enthusiasts, Santiago offers dedicated facilities. Panul Park, known for its native forest, has trails for various skill levels. El Durazno Bike Park is another popular spot, featuring routes and jumps of different difficulties for those looking for more technical challenges.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on touring cycling routes around Santiago?
A: When cycling in the Andes Mountains, particularly on higher routes, you might have the chance to spot condors soaring overhead. The diverse landscapes, from mountains to valleys, also support various native flora and fauna, though specific wildlife sightings can vary depending on the route and season.
tips: Beautiful landscape with some old villas with fantastic architecture

I advise you to take a break to admire the turquoise tones of the sea due to the presence of sandbanks and islets.

But the view is fabulous and very beautiful.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "Hard bike ride" and demands very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of over 1,000 meters. While some sources might suggest it's suitable for all skill levels, the substantial ascent makes it best suited for experienced touring cyclists accustomed to climbs.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Circular Tour 1 of Sesimbra?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, which is typical for touring cycling. However, as it passes through the broader Arrábida Natural Park, you might encounter a mix of asphalt and some gravel roads. Expect challenging uphills, particularly through the mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking and diverse scenery. Key highlights include the stunning views from Panoramic Descent with View of Tróia Peninsula and Setúbal, the challenging but rewarding Climb of the Arrábida Coastal Road, and the historic Sesimbra Castle. The route also offers fantastic views of the blue sea, dramatic cliffs, and lush Mediterranean vegetation within Arrábida Natural Park.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 53.5-kilometer touring cycling route is approximately 3 hours and 53 minutes. However, this can vary depending on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Arrábida Natural Park offers suitable conditions for cycling throughout the year. However, the period from September to June is generally recommended to avoid the highest summer temperatures and increased visitor numbers on the beaches.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Circular Tour 1 of Sesimbra?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the start of Circular Tour 1 are not provided, Sesimbra offers various parking options, especially near the town center or along the waterfront. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Arrábida Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and designated trails within Arrábida Natural Park. However, as a protected biogenetic reserve, it's important to respect all park regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Is the Circular Tour 1 of Sesimbra — Arrábida Natural Park dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Arrábida Natural Park generally allows dogs, specific rules may apply to certain trails or protected areas. For cycling, dogs should always be kept on a leash and under control, especially when sharing paths with others or in areas with wildlife. It's best to check local park guidelines for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route - part Portugal 8, Caminho do Atlântico (Troço Concelho Sesimbra), and Aldeias de Azeitão (Longo).

faqs.question: What makes this touring cycling route particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its unique combination of a significant physical challenge with some of Portugal's most spectacular natural landscapes. It offers diverse ecosystems, from panoramic ocean views to Mediterranean scrubland, and is easily accessible from Lisbon, making it a rewarding escape for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any specific road closures or restrictions to be aware of, especially along the coastal sections?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally open, certain coastal roads within Arrábida Natural Park, particularly those leading to popular beaches, may have seasonal restrictions or limited access for vehicles, including bicycles, during peak summer months or special events. It's always a good idea to check local advisories before your trip, especially if planning to cycle between June and August.
